title: $log10
text: $log10 Return the base 10 logarithm of X (log 10 X). $log10 ( X ) Parameters X — positive numeric constant, or a field (or indirect reference to a field), variable, function, or expression that evaluates to a positive numeric value. X must be in the range 0 through 10 9999 . Return Values Base 10 logarithm of X If an error occurs, $procerror contains a negative value that identifies the exact error. Table 1. Values of $procerror Commonly Returned Following $log and $log10 . Value Error constant Meaning -1204 <UPROCERR_NEGATIVE> Negative value not allowed. -1205 <UPROCERR_ZERO> Zero value not allowed. Use Allowed in all component types. Description The function $log10 returns the base 10 logarithm of X , that is, log 10 X . Example: Using $log10 The following example returns the base 10 logarithm of the given expression: $NOTNATLOG$ = $log10(MYFIELD * 2) Copy If the value of MYFIELD is 50, the value stored in $NOTNATLOG$ is 2. Related Links $log Return the natural logarithm of X (log e X). $exp10 Return the base 10 exponential of X (10X). Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
